---
title: "Housing Precarity and Displacement"
subtitle: "Racial and Gender Inequality in Gentrification and Eviction"
toc: true
author-title: Instructor
author:
  - "Tim Thomas, Ph.D."
  - <timthomas@berkeley.edu>
---
Sociology N100, Sec 002 (Class #13195) | 2 units | Summer Session D | Online via Zoom\
Tuesdays & Thursdays | 5-7pm | July 7 to August 13, 2026\
[Class Zoom Link](https://berkeley.zoom.us/j/92041866237)\
[bCourse Site](https://bcourses.berkeley.edu/courses/1555635)\
[Class Discussion Page](https://bcourses.berkeley.edu/courses/1555635/discussion_topics)

**Office Hours**: Schedule a time with me at [cal.com/timthomas](https://cal.com/timthomas). Evenings and weekends are also available by email request.

## Week 1
#### Tuesday July 7 - Lecture
* Course introduction and [syllabus](syllabus.qmd)
* How to use AI in this course - discussion and live demo

#### Thursday July 9 - Lab
* Lab 1: getting started in RStudio on the DataHub (we walk through it together online), intro to R, and your first Census number
* AI tutorial: debugging with AI, verifying answers, and sharing your conversation link

#### Set up this week
* Get a free census API key at <https://api.census.gov/data/key_signup.html> (a couple minutes); we use it in lab.
* Set up a free account on an AI assistant that can share a public conversation link (Perplexity recommended; ChatGPT, Gemini, or Claude also work). Sign in with a **personal** account, not your CalNet login, so that link-sharing works. [See the AI policy for details.](syllabus.html#ai-policy)

## Week 2

#### Readings
***Due Tuesday July 14***

* [Zuk et al. (2015) "Gentrification, Displacement and the Role of Public Investment: A Literature Review"](https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Karen-Chapple/publication/334638363_Gentrification_Displacement_and_the_Role_of_Public_Investment_A_Literature_Review/links/5d37697f4585153e591c453f/Gentrification-Displacement-and-the-Role-of-Public-Investment-A-Literature-Review.pdf)

***Due Thursday July 16***

* [Walker (2023) “Analyzing US Census Data: Methods, Maps, and Models in R”: Preface, Ch 1, & Ch 2](https://walker-data.com/census-r/index.html)

#### Tuesday July 14 - Lecture

#### Thursday July 16 - Lab

## Week 3
#### Readings
***Due before lab, Thursday July 23***

* [Walker (2023) Chapter 3 & Chapter 4](https://walker-data.com/census-r/wrangling-census-data-with-tidyverse-tools.html)

#### Tuesday July 21 - Lab

#### Thursday July 23 - Lab - working with Census data cont.

#### **Assignment 1** - *Due Monday, July 27 @5pm*

A short solo warm-up, and the first step of a thread you carry through Assignment 2 and your final project. Pick a county, set of counties, or region you care about - you will keep working with this same area all term.

1. Use `get_acs()` to pull one ACS variable that says something about housing precarity (for example, rent burden, median rent, or the share of households that rent).
2. Make one chart of it (a bar chart across your counties works well).
3. Write two or three sentences describing what the chart shows and anything that surprised you.

Submit your R script, the chart, your sentences, and the share link(s) to any AI you used. Keep it simple - no bibliography or ASA formatting needed yet. Submit to bCourses.

## Week 4

#### Readings

***Due Thursday July 30***
[Walker (2023) Chapters 5 & 6](https://walker-data.com/census-r/census-geographic-data-and-applications-in-r.html)

#### Tuesday July 28 - Lab - pulling in external datasets

#### Thursday July 30 - Lab

* [Review San Diego Displacement Study](https://urban-displacement.github.io/edr-sandag/)

## Week 5

#### **Assignment 2** - *Due Monday, August 3 @5pm*

Build directly on Assignment 1: stay with the same area and go deeper. This becomes the seed of your final group project.

Add two or three more ACS variables and combine them into a measure of displacement risk, inequality, or some other housing precarity measure using the American Community Survey. Include descriptive statistics, plots, and an interpretation of what you found. End with a draft research question and at least two hypotheses you could test about the disparate impact of displacement in your area. Submit to bCourses.

For this assignment you will submit:

1. R code script that is replicable by the grader,

2. a brief writeup explaining your assignment with plots and context,
    * Include one or more introductory paragraphs summarizing what you are analyzing and your overall findings.
    * Text and plots walking the reader through a more detailed understanding of what you found. Make sure to describe your plots.
    * A draft research question and at least two hypotheses for your final project.

3. Bibliography and inline citations in ASA (American Sociological Association) format.

4. List of URL links to any and all AI usage (use any approved AI tool that can share a public conversation link).
    * In your code, put the URL in comments next to the relevant area.
    * In your writeup, put the URL in footnotes near the section or text in which you used AI. [See AI policy in the Syllabus for more info.](syllabus.html#ai-policy)


These assignments will be graded on the [scale in the syllabus](syllabus.html#course-grading). This grade is to help you see the quality of your work but the final grade will be graded as complete or incomplete. No late assignments will be accepted except for special accommodations. You can volunteer to present a 5-minute demo of your assignment in class.

#### Readings

***Due Tuesday August 4***
[Bates (2013) "Gentrification and Displacement Study: Implementing an Equitable Inclusive Development Strategy in the Context of Gentrification"](https://www.portland.gov/sites/default/files/2020-01/2-gentrification-and-displacement-study-05.18.13.pdf)

#### Tuesday August 4 - Lecture

* Form randomly assigned groups of 2 or 3 and discussion on final group project
* Draft some ideas on your final group project
    * Include what area you want to study (at least two counties or a region),
    * what you want to explore about displacement (at least 3-5 different data points/plots),
    * what data you will use to capture this.
* Present your idea to the group and get a sign-off from Tim

#### Thursday August 6 - Lab

## Week 6
#### Tuesday August 11 - Group Lab

* [How to make an effective presentation video](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/12WupIg1R57WOgEjr2yfvfYm-QoJy1mOE?usp=drive_link)

* Example presentation: [Tim's Testimony Slides to the WA State Senate](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1x7SCdTeBdczY5Ga5wvixMxtvTv-a4rTph6LIQWLsIk0/edit?pli=1&slide=id.g32100058b3b_1_1#slide=id.g32100058b3b_1_1)
    * [Recorded testimony (starts at 40 minutes)](https://tvw.org/video/senate-housing-2024121118/)

#### Thursday August 13 - Presentation

* 10-15 minute presentation on your research. No more than 15 slides and aim for 5.

Final Assignment Due Friday August 14th
